# Release upgrade fixtures

These SQLite files contain **schema only**. Each was generated by checking out
the named release tag and running that tag's own SQLAlchemy metadata:

```python
from app.database import Base, engine
import app.models
import app.painting.models

Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
```

`test_release_upgrade_matrix.py` copies a fixture, stamps the Alembic revision
that shipped with that release, inserts representative user data, and runs the
current application's real startup migration path twice. Keeping seed data in
the test makes the binary fixtures free of personal paths, credentials, and
other user data.

| Fixture | Source tag | Starting revision |
|---|---|---|
| `v0.18.0.db` | `v0.18.0` | `0027` |
| `v0.19.0.db` | `v0.19.0` | `0028` |
| `v0.20.0.db` | `v0.20.0` | `0032` |

When adding a release-line fixture, generate it from the tagged source rather
than today's ORM models. Revision `0001` is a no-op baseline, so reconstructing
an old database from current metadata would not test the historical upgrade
path.
